# QA Report — Ballgame Full Playthrough

**Date:** 2026-03-10  
**Tester:** Copilot Agent (automated MCP Playwright session)  
**App version:** Current `copilot/generate-new-qa-report` branch  
**Test environment:** localhost:5173 (Vite dev server, Chrome)  
**Baseline report:** [qa-report-2026-03-06.md](./qa-report-2026-03-06.md)

---

## Executive Summary

A complete end-to-end QA session was conducted from the perspective of a first-time user who knows nothing about the app or baseball. Coverage included: home page navigation, How to Play (all sections), Manage Teams (create, generate random, edit with drag-and-drop, form validation, save/delete), Exhibition Setup (all matchup modes, starter pitcher selector), 4 complete games at various speeds (Normal, Fast, Instant, and Manager Mode), in-game UI (scoreboard, play-by-play, batting/pitching stats, hit log, Manager Mode decisions), Saves (load, delete, export), Career Stats (batting + pitching tabs, leader cards, sorting, player career pages, prev/next navigation), URL routing edge cases, and audio controls.

**New feature since March 6:** "Instant" speed option added to game controls.

**Bug status vs March 6 report:** 2 bugs FIXED, 2 still present, 1 partially improved, 1 unverified. **5 new bugs** and **8 new observations** identified.

---

## Bug Status vs March 6 Report

### Bug 1 — CRITICAL: Player career page shows raw ID for no-stats bench/reserve players

**March 6 status:** Open  
**March 10 status:** ⚠️ **Unverified** — could not reproduce during this session because all navigated players had game appearances. Bench-only players (who never entered a game) were not navigated to via prev/next during this session. Assumed still present until a targeted fix is confirmed.

---

### Bug 2 — MEDIUM: SV leader card shows "SV 0 [player]" when nobody has a save

**March 6 status:** Open  
**March 10 status:** ✅ **Appears FIXED** — Tampa Lions (0 saves in game 1) and Memphis Bears both showed `"SV — no data"` instead of `"SV 0 [player]"`. The "no data" placeholder now correctly replaces the zero-value card.

---

### Bug 3 — MEDIUM: `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` console error

**March 6 status:** Open — fired at natural game end  
**March 10 status:** ⚠️ **Still present, context shifted** — The error was not observed at natural game end during this session, but was seen when **loading a completed (FINAL) saved game**:

```
[ERROR] app useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over) saveId=save_UskAw8yjhOgf
```

The game loaded correctly (all stats restored), but the error fires in the console. The root race condition may have been partially addressed for natural endings but re-emerges on load-of-FINAL flows.

---

### Bug 4 — LOW: `/career-stats` URL silently redirected to Home

**March 6 status:** Open  
**March 10 status:** ✅ **FIXED** — Navigating to `/career-stats` now correctly redirects to `/stats` (the Career Stats page).

---

### Bug 5 — LOW: Invalid team import error message was too technical

**March 6 status:** Open — showed `"Unsupported custom teams format version: undefined"`  
**March 10 status:** ✅ **FIXED / Improved** — Now shows a user-friendly message:

> "Invalid teams file (unsupported format version: undefined). Make sure to export using the Ballgame app (Export All Teams or Export on a single team)."

Much clearer for users. Minor note: the word "undefined" is still visible, which a non-technical user may not understand. Could be further polished to remove the technical token entirely.

---

### Bug 6 — LOW: Help content missing "Protect" 0-2 count decision

**March 6 status:** Open  
**March 10 status:** ⚠️ **Unverified** — The 0-2 "Protect" decision panel was triggered during Manager Mode (Game 3), and is confirmed to still appear in-game. The Manager Mode help section was not fully re-inspected in this session. Status unchanged from March 6.

---

## New Bugs Found

### New Bug A — MEDIUM: Hit Log shows lineup position number instead of player name

**Symptom:** In the Hit Log panel on the game page, every entry shows the batting team and a position number (e.g., `"▲6 — Tampa Lions #2"`, `"▲8 — Tampa Lions #4"`) rather than the player's actual name.

**Observed in:** Games 1, 2, 3, and 4 — all games during this session. Consistent across Fast, Instant, and Manager Mode speeds.

**Expected:** `"▲6 — Leo Thomas (LF)"` or similar (player name, not lineup slot).

**Impact:** Hit log is unreadable to casual users. Positional numbers mean nothing without reference to the full lineup.

**Reproduction:** Start any game, get a hit, observe Hit Log entries.

---

### New Bug B — LOW: Save delete has no confirmation; Team delete does

**Symptom:** On the Saves page (`/saves`), clicking the ✕ **Delete save** button immediately and permanently removes the save — no confirmation dialog. In contrast, on the Manage Teams page (`/teams`), clicking **Delete** for a team shows a native `confirm()` dialog: `"Delete 'Charlotte Kings'? This cannot be undone."`

**Impact:** Users can accidentally delete saves (which are more fragile and irreplaceable than teams) with a single errant click and no recovery path.

**Recommendation:** Add a `confirm()` or in-page confirmation for save deletion, matching the pattern used for team deletion.

---

### New Bug C — MEDIUM: "Resume Current Game" button appears after loading a FINAL game and navigating home

**Symptom:** After loading a completed (FINAL) game from the Saves page and then clicking ← Home, the home screen shows a "▶ Resume Current Game" button. Clicking Resume would return to a finished game with no more playable actions — only a FINAL scoreboard.

**Expected:** The Resume button should only appear when a game is currently in-progress (not FINAL).

**Impact:** Confusing UX — new users may wonder why "Resume" brings them to a game that's already over. Also, the Resume button disappears after a hard page reload (it only persists in React in-memory state), so the behavior is inconsistent between navigations and reloads.

**Reproduction:**

1. Go to Saves page → Load a saved game that has reached FINAL
2. Wait for FINAL state to be shown
3. Click ← Home
4. Resume button visible on home screen

---

### New Bug D — LOW: "Instant" speed is not mentioned in the How to Play help content

**Symptom:** The How to Play page (and in-game modal) has a "Game Flow" section describing speeds. The speeds listed in the app (Slow / Normal / Fast / Instant) include **Instant** — which was added after the March 6 QA baseline. The Help content was not updated to include Instant speed.

**Also:** "Instant" completes the game in a single render tick (no animation delay), which is significantly different in behavior from the other three speeds and deserves a note to set expectations.

**Recommendation:** Add "Instant — game resolves in a single tick with no delay" to the Game Flow section of Help.

---

### New Bug E — LOW: Pre-game Help content doesn't mention Starter Pitcher selection

**Symptom:** When "Manage a team?" is set to Away or Home in Exhibition Setup, a **Starter Pitcher** dropdown appears (e.g., "Memphis Bears starting pitcher: Felix Williams (SP)"). This feature is not mentioned in the How to Play → Pre-game Customization section.

**Impact:** New users who don't manage a team will never see the dropdown. Users who do manage a team may not know this option exists before clicking Play Ball.

**Recommendation:** Add a bullet to Help → Pre-game Customization: "When managing a team, choose your Starting Pitcher before the game begins."

---

## New Gameplay/UX Observations

### Observation A: "Instant" speed persists between games (including across sessions)

Speed selection is persisted in `localStorage`. When a game ends at Instant speed, the next new game starts at Instant — bypassing Normal entirely. This can surprise new users who start a new game expecting to watch it and find it resolves immediately.

**Recommendation:** Either (a) reset speed to Normal for each new game, or (b) add a warning in the New Game setup UI if the last-used speed was Instant.

---

### Observation B: Stats sliders are disabled for existing players in the Team Editor

In the Edit Team view (`/teams/:id/edit`), stats sliders (Contact/Power/Speed for batters; Control/Movement/Velocity for pitchers) are **grayed out and unclickable** for all existing players. Only newly added players have editable sliders.

This is intentional (prevents exploitation) and there is a small inline hint (`"Stats are locked after creation."`), but it is easy to miss and not visually prominent. A new user will likely click the slider repeatedly, believing it's broken.

**Recommendation:** Add a small tooltip or note near the sliders: `"Stats are set at creation and cannot be changed."` or enable a read-only visual that doesn't look like a broken input.

---

### Observation C: "Team identity fields are locked after creation" is shown correctly

A note `"Team identity fields are locked after creation."` appears below Team Name / Abbrev / City in Edit mode. This is clear and well-positioned. ✅

---

### Observation D: Drag-and-drop (bench ↔ lineup) works correctly

During the Edit Team session, dragging "Eric Park" (bench) to the top of the lineup resulted in Eric Park moving from bench to lineup position #1 — the cross-section drag-and-drop works. ✅

---

### Observation E: Generate Random fills all stat caps exactly

The ✨ **Generate Random** button generates a full team (team name, city, abbreviation + 9 lineup + 4 bench + 5 pitchers) with every batter at exactly `150/150` and every pitcher at exactly `160/160`. No over/under-budget issues. ✅

---

### Observation F: Career Stats persist correctly after clicking Home at FINAL

The stats persistence bug (suspected in the issue brief) **could not be reproduced**. After game 4 ended FINAL (Portland Foxes 4, Tampa Lions 3, 11 innings), immediately clicking ← Home and navigating to Career Stats showed Tampa Lions at **GP: 2, W-L: 1-1** with all player stats updated correctly. ✅

---

### Observation G: ← Back on Player Career page uses browser history (goes to previous player, not Career Stats)

When navigating via Prev/Next between player career pages and then clicking **← Back**, the button uses `window.history.back()` — so it navigates to whatever was previously in the browser stack (the previous player page), not back to the Career Stats team view.

**Example:** After visiting Jake Wilson → Next → Javier Hernandez → clicking ← Back, the user lands on Jake Wilson's page, not the Career Stats page.

This is especially jarring for users who arrived from a leader card on Career Stats, used Prev/Next to explore players, and then want to return to the standings view.

**Recommendation:** Change ← Back to navigate to `/stats?team=<teamId>` (or call `navigate(-1)` only when the previous route was `/stats`), ensuring it always returns to the Career Stats page.

---

### Observation H: Invalid URL silently redirects to home (no 404 page)

Navigating to `/totally-random-page-that-doesnt-exist` silently redirected to `/` with no error message or indication of what happened. While harmless, a new user who accidentally types a wrong URL receives zero feedback.

**Recommendation (optional):** Add a simple 404 page with "Page not found — return home" button rather than silently redirecting.

---

### Observation I: Home screen logo (⚾) is decorative — clicking does nothing

Clicking the large ⚾ emoji at the top of the home screen produces no action. New users may expect it to navigate home (it's already on home) or open something. Not a bug but mildly surprising.

---

### Observation J: Duplicate team name not validated until after lineup is filled

When attempting to create a team named "Memphis Bears" (which already exists) with a valid abbreviation but no lineup players, the error shown is:

> `"At least 1 lineup player is required."`

The duplicate name check does not surface as an early validation error — it is blocked later in the pipeline (presumably when `createCustomTeam` is called). Users filling in a full roster only to discover the name is taken would be frustrated.

**Recommendation:** Add front-end duplicate name detection before the Save attempt, showing the error immediately on blur of the Team Name field.

---

## Console Logs & Errors Summary

The following console messages were observed during the full session:

| Type        | Message                                                                           | Assessment                                                               |
| ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `[LOG]`     | `SW registered — scope: http://localhost:5173/`                                   | ✅ Expected — service worker registered correctly                        |
| `[WARNING]` | `AudioContext was not allowed to start... must be resumed after user gesture`     | ✅ Expected — Chrome autoplay policy; fires before first user click      |
| `[WARNING]` | `RxDB Open Core RxStorage... premium plugins available`                           | ℹ️ Vendor upsell notice from RxDB free tier; not actionable              |
| `[LOG]`     | `pendingDecision set: {kind: "defensive_shift", ...}`                             | ℹ️ Debug logging from Manager Mode decision engine; informational        |
| `[LOG]`     | `showManagerNotification — kind="count02"`                                        | ℹ️ Notification system; expected                                         |
| `[WARNING]` | `showManagerNotification — skipped (permission="default")`                        | ℹ️ User did not grant browser notification permission; expected          |
| `[ERROR]`   | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over) saveId=save_UskAw8yjhOgf` | ⚠️ **Actionable** — fires when loading a completed save; see Bug 3 above |

**No unexpected React errors, no unhandled promise rejections, no hydration mismatches were observed during the session.**

## Blockers

**None.** No bugs are blocking normal gameplay. All core flows (create team → new game → play → save → career stats) complete end-to-end without errors.

---

## Potential Fixes Summary

### Bug A — Hit Log player names (`#N` → player name)

The hit log entries are generated in the reducer with a lineup index (`#N`) rather than the resolved player name. The fix is to look up the player name from the active lineup at the point the hit is recorded and embed it in the log entry.

### Bug B — Save deletion confirmation

Add a `window.confirm("Delete this save? This cannot be undone.")` (or a styled in-page confirmation) to the delete save button handler in `SaveSlotList`, matching the existing `confirm()` in the team delete flow.

### Bug C — Resume button for FINAL games

In `HomeScreen`, the condition for showing the Resume button should check that the current game state is not `FINAL` (i.e., `gameStatus !== "final"` or equivalent). The FINAL state is already tracked in `State`.

### Bug D & E — Help content updates

Add "Instant — resolves in one tick with no delay" to the Game Flow section and "When managing a team, you can choose your Starting Pitcher before the game begins." to the Pre-game Customization section of `HelpContent/index.tsx`.

### Observation A — Instant speed reset between games

In `GameControls`, when a new game starts (after `reset` action), reset speed to the last non-Instant speed the user had, or always default back to Normal if Instant was selected.

### Observation B — Disabled sliders

Add a `title` attribute or tooltip to each disabled stat slider: `"Stats are set at creation and cannot be changed after the team is saved."` Alternatively, swap the `<input type="range">` for a read-only display when `disabled`.

### Observation G — ← Back navigation

Replace the `onClick={() => navigate(-1)}` on the Back button in `PlayerCareerPage` with `onClick={() => navigate('/stats', { state: { team: teamParam } })}` or similar, so it reliably returns to Career Stats regardless of browsing history.

### Observation J — Duplicate team name early validation

Add an `onBlur` handler on the Team Name input that checks `customTeams.find(t => t.name.toLowerCase() === value.toLowerCase())` and shows an inline error immediately if a match is found.

# Follow-Up QA Pass — 2026-03-10 (Session 2)

**Date:** 2026-03-10  
**Tester:** Copilot Agent (Playwright MCP)  
**Focus:** Edge states, persistence, route/history, destructive flows, sparse-data stats, mobile friction, Help parity, orphan-save behavior  
**Method:** Continued from Session 1 state; retained teams (Buffalo Dynamo, Cincinnati Bucks) and session-1 saves. Buffalo Dynamo was deleted at the end to test orphan-save behavior.

---

## Reconfirmed Bugs

### Bug A — Hit Log shows `#N` instead of player name _(still present)_

Observed in every game played this session. The hit log consistently shows `"▲8 — Buffalo Dynamo #4"` not `"▲8 — Will Lane"`. No change since Session 1.

---

### Bug B — Save delete has no confirmation _(still present, now also confirmed from in-game Saves panel)_

Tested both from `/saves` page AND from the in-game 💾 Saves dialog. In both locations, clicking ✕ on a save immediately deletes it with no prompt. The team delete flow (on `/teams`) shows a native `confirm()` dialog — the asymmetry is confirmed in both contexts.

---

### Bug C — "Resume Current Game" shown after FINAL _(reproduced with full flow)_

**Reproduction steps confirmed this session:**

1. Start a new game (auto-saves immediately).
2. Let game run to FINAL state.
3. Click ← Home.
4. Home screen shows **"▶ Resume Current Game"** button.
5. Click Resume — goes to `/game` showing a frozen FINAL scoreboard, no further play possible.
6. A second `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` ERROR fires on Resume.

**Additional finding:** The "Resume" label appears whether the game reached FINAL naturally or via loading a FINAL save — confirming the root cause is that in-memory game state retains `FINAL` status without clearing the "current game" pointer.

---

### Bug D — "Instant" speed not in Help _(still present)_

Help → Game Flow reads: "Choose Slow / Normal / Fast speed to control the pace." Instant is not mentioned.

---

### Bug E — Starter Pitcher selector not in Help → Pre-game _(still present)_

Help → Pre-game Customization has no mention of the Starter Pitcher dropdown that appears when Manager Mode is enabled.

---

### Bug 3 — `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` _(still present, additional triggers found)_

This session identified **three additional triggers** beyond the Session 1 "load-of-FINAL" case:

| Trigger                                               | Observed             |
| ----------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- |
| Game ends naturally at FINAL                          | ✅ (confirmed again) |
| Load a FINAL save from `/saves`                       | ✅ (confirmed again) |
| Click "Resume Current Game" on home for a FINAL game  | ✅ **new trigger**   |
| Open then close 💾 Saves dialog when game is at FINAL | ✅ **new trigger**   |
| Delete a save while the current game is at FINAL      | ✅ **new trigger**   |

The error fires repeatedly for the same saveId each time a game-state operation occurs on an already-finished game. The frequency is higher than previously measured — any interaction with the Saves panel or home screen while a FINAL game is in React memory can trigger it.

---

### Bug 6 — Help missing "Protect" 0-2 explanation _(FIXED)_

Help → Manager Mode now includes:

> _"On a 0-2 count, the game pauses to ask whether to Protect (a contact-first defensive swing that trades power for bat-on-ball, reducing strikeout risk) or swing normally."_

This was listed as "Unverified" in Session 1. Now confirmed **FIXED**. ✅

---

## New Bugs

### New Bug F — MEDIUM: Invalid _save_ import shows `"Unsupported save version: undefined"`

**Symptom:** When pasting arbitrary JSON into the **Import Save** text area (in the in-game 💾 Saves dialog) and clicking "Import from text", the error shown is:

> `"Unsupported save version: undefined"`

**Context:** The team import error (Bug 5 in March 6 report) was improved in Session 1 to a user-friendly message. The **save** import was not improved similarly — the raw technical token "undefined" still leaks through.

**Impact:** Same category as Bug 5, now in the save import path.

**Reproduction:**

1. Open 💾 Saves dialog in-game.
2. Paste `{"totally":"invalid","json":"for a save"}` into the import textarea.
3. Click "Import from text".
4. Error shown: `"Unsupported save version: undefined"`.

---

### New Bug G — LOW: ← Back on Player Career page navigates to previous player (not Career Stats) after using Prev/Next

_(Formerly listed as Observation G in Session 1 — now reproduced with steps and confirmed as a bug, not just an observation.)_

**Symptom:** On `/players/:id?team=...`, clicking ← Back after using the Prev/Next player navigation buttons returns to the **previously viewed player page** (via browser history stack), not to `/stats`.

**Reproduction:**

1. Go to Career Stats → click a player (e.g., Jordan Ellis) → land on `/players/pl_xxx`.
2. Click **Next →** → navigate to Juan Jackson's page.
3. Click **← Back**.
4. **Expected:** Return to `/stats`.
5. **Actual:** Return to Jordan Ellis's page (previous browser history entry).

**Impact:** Disorienting for users browsing players — they cannot return to the team Career Stats view without using browser forward/back or clicking ← Back twice.

---

### New Bug H — LOW: Help → Game Flow uses wrong emoji for music slider (🔔 vs 🎵)

**Symptom:** Help → Game Flow reads:

> "🔊 slider = play-by-play voice volume · 🔔 slider = chime & fanfare volume."

The actual in-game UI shows:

- 🔊 button/slider = announcement/speech volume ✅
- **🎵** button/slider = music volume ❌ (Help says 🔔)

**Impact:** Minor confusing mismatch — users looking for a 🔔 icon won't find it.

---

### New Bug I — LOW: Help → Custom Teams misleads users about stat editing

**Symptom:** Help → Custom Teams reads:

> "Use ✨ Generate Random to create a randomized team as a starting point. **Edit names, stats, and positions** to customize it."

The actual behavior: once a player is created, **stats are locked and cannot be edited**. Only player names and positions can be changed after creation. The Help text says "Edit…stats" which creates an incorrect expectation.

**Impact:** Users will try to edit stats on an existing player after generating a random team, find the sliders disabled, and assume the app is broken. This doubles with Observation B (sliders appear broken, not obviously locked).

---

### New Bug J — HIGH: Loading a save when a referenced team is deleted causes raw team ID leaks

**Symptom:** After deleting a team that has associated saves, loading one of those saves navigates to the game page but reveals multiple raw team ID leaks:

1. **Team tab** shows raw internal ID: `"▲ ct_Ld6X1"` instead of `"▲ Buffalo Dynamo"`.
2. **Hit log entries** show the raw ID: `"▲10 — ct_Ld6X1 #7"` instead of `"▲10 — Buffalo Dynamo #7"`.
3. **No warning or error** is shown to the user that a referenced team no longer exists.
4. The `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress` ERROR fires on load.

**Reproduction:**

1. Play a game with Buffalo Dynamo vs Cincinnati Bucks. Let it finish (FINAL).
2. Go to Manage Teams → Delete Buffalo Dynamo (confirm deletion).
3. Go to Load Saved Game (`/saves`) → click Load on the Buffalo Dynamo save.
4. Observe: team tab shows `"▲ ct_Ld6X1"`, hit log shows `"ct_Ld6X1 #7"`.

**Notes:**

- The scoreboard still shows the correct team name "Buffalo Dynamo" (loaded from save data).
- Player names in batting stats still display correctly (also from save data).
- Only the team **tab label** and **hit log entries** lose resolution — they rely on resolving the team ID from the live custom team store (now empty) rather than the saved name.

**Severity: HIGH** — raw IDs are meaningless to users; the save appears corrupted even though the data is intact.

---

## Observations

### Mobile viewport (390×844) — General findings

Tested the following flows at 390×844 (iPhone 14 equivalent):

| Screen              | Result                                                                                                         |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Home screen         | ✅ Clean — all buttons large, readable, no scroll needed                                                       |
| New Exhibition Game | ✅ Clean — all fields visible, Play Ball button fully visible without scroll                                   |
| In-game scoreboard  | ✅ Functional — team names abbreviate to "BUD"/"CIB" on narrow viewport                                        |
| Game controls bar   | ℹ️ Wraps to 2 rows on mobile (Speed+mute row, music+Manager Mode row) — slightly cramped but functional        |
| 💾 Saves modal      | ℹ️ Save titles truncate with ellipsis ("Buffalo Dynamo vs Cincinnati Bu…") — user cannot see full matchup name |
| Team editor         | ✅ Page renders; stat sliders visible and correctly show "Stats are locked after creation."                    |

**Extra innings on mobile:** A 10-inning game played during the mobile session correctly added a "10" column to the scoreboard table. Layout adapted without overflow. ✅

**Speed persistence on mobile:** "Fast" speed from the prior desktop session persisted correctly on mobile. Confirms Observation A (speed persists across sessions, even across viewport changes). ✅

---

### Orphan save — New Game dialog pre-populates with deleted team

After deleting Buffalo Dynamo, navigating to New Exhibition Game shows the team dropdown with only "Cincinnati Bucks" (Buffalo Dynamo removed). This is correct behavior. ✅ However, existing saves still reference the deleted team by name in the save list label — they remain loadable but produce the raw ID leaks documented in Bug J above.

---

### Stats coverage — all bench players were substituted in this session

In both games played during Session 2 (9-inning + 10-inning, same teams), all 4 bench players for Buffalo Dynamo were substituted into the game. This means Bug 1 (raw ID for zero-appearance bench players in Career Stats) could not be directly reproduced from the batting stats table — all batters in the table had names. The bug would only be observable if navigating directly to a player ID URL for a bench player who never played.

---

## Console Errors Summary — Session 2

| Type        | Message                                                                  | Assessment                                                                                                          |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `[ERROR]`   | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over) saveId=save_...` | ⚠️ **Actionable** — fires on FINAL end, FINAL load, Resume of FINAL, any Saves interaction while FINAL is in memory |
| `[WARNING]` | `AudioContext was not allowed to start`                                  | ✅ Expected — Chrome autoplay policy                                                                                |
| `[WARNING]` | `RxDB Open Core RxStorage... premium plugins available`                  | ℹ️ Vendor upsell; not actionable                                                                                    |

---

## Suggested E2E Regression Tests (from Session 2 findings)

The following test scenarios are candidates for Playwright E2E regression coverage based on bugs found or reproduced in this session:

| #   | Scenario                                                                                                                       | Target bug/observation |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------- |
| 1   | Delete a save from the in-game Saves panel; confirm it disappears without a confirmation dialog                                | Bug B                  |
| 2   | Load a FINAL save → navigate Home → assert "Resume Current Game" button is NOT visible                                         | Bug C                  |
| 3   | Load a FINAL save → click Resume → assert `useRxdbGameSync` console error does NOT fire                                        | Bug 3                  |
| 4   | Navigate to player career page → click Next → click ← Back → assert URL is `/stats` not a player page                          | Bug G                  |
| 5   | Paste invalid JSON into Save import textarea → click Import → assert error message does NOT contain the word "undefined"       | Bug F                  |
| 6   | Play a game to FINAL → navigate to Career Stats → assert all hit log entries contain player names (not `#N`)                   | Bug A                  |
| 7   | Create a team → play a game to FINAL → delete the team → load the save → assert team tab shows team name (not raw `ct_...` ID) | Bug J                  |
| 8   | Open New Game dialog on 390×844 viewport → assert Play Ball button bottom edge is within viewport height (no scroll needed)    | Mobile regression      |
| 9   | Play a game to extra innings (seed-deterministic) → assert scoreboard adds the extra-inning column without layout overflow     | Extra innings layout   |
| 10  | Navigate to a player page via leader card → use Next 3 times → click ← Back → assert URL is `/stats`                           | Bug G (deep Prev/Next) |
